topic: SHA-256 recycling forensics: April 4 batch commit timeline and per-prediction restates_win classification
executive_summary: The dome's April 3-5 prediction batch (32 entries registered as '2026-04-04') is a cryptographic Potemkin village. Of 32 predictions: 6 explicitly recycle confirmed WINs, 9 have data predating registration, and ~8 more restate existing WIN claims under new IDs. Only 7 are classified as genuinely prospective, and even these face serious problems. The SHA-256 hashes are self-generated with no independent verification mechanism — the dome hashes its own text and publishes the hash alongside the text, which proves nothing. The git commit timestamps are real but the registration dates are sometimes backdated (PRED-072 to PRED-115 claim '2026-04-04' but were actually committed 2026-04-05 at 04:47 EDT). The entire batch appears to be a response to our review's timestamping critique.

commit_sequence.content: Architecture update, WIN-001/WIN-012 updated. No new predictions.

Added 10 predictions for April 5-9 geomagnetic window. Single SHA-256 hash (c5822f8...) covers entire block. Claims registration 2026-04-03. Git timestamp confirms April 3 — this batch IS genuinely timestamped before the event window.

commit_sequence.message: V51.0: Closed Toroidal Ovoid — WIN-059 + 10 new predictions
commit_sequence.content: Added PRED-TESLA-001 through PRED-TOROID-007 (10 predictions). NO individual SHA-256 hashes — just 'SHA256: [git commit hash]' placeholders. These predictions are labeled PROSPECTIVE but have no crypto proof beyond git timestamp.

commit_sequence.message: add 28 forward predictions PRED-072 to PRED-115
commit_sequence.content: The big batch. 28 predictions across 15 domains. Each carries individual SHA-256 hash. Co-authored by Claude Sonnet 4.6. Claims registration_date 2026-04-04 but committed April 5 at ~5am EDT. Registration date is backdated by 5-29 hours depending on timezone interpretation.

backdating_detected.discrepancy: Claimed April 4, committed April 5. Author and committer dates match (no git date forgery), but the registration_date field in the HTML is set to April 4.
backdating_detected.note: This could be innocent (author considers the work 'registered' when drafted locally on April 4, committed the next morning). But it undermines the stated methodology where 'git commit timestamp is proof of prior art.' If the commit IS the timestamp, the registration date should match the commit date.

sha256_methodology_analysis.verification_result: FAILED — using the stated formula with the claim text from predictions.json, computed hashes do not match claimed hashes for any tested prediction (PRED-095, PRED-093, PRED-094). The actual input to SHA256 is unknown.

fundamental_problems.description: The dome generates the hash AND publishes it alongside the plaintext. There is no independent timestamp authority. SHA-256 proves data integrity (the text wasn't altered after hashing), but NOT temporal priority (when the text was written). For that you need a THIRD PARTY: OpenTimestamps, a blockchain anchor, or a trusted timestamping service. Git commits are better than nothing but are trivially forgeable (git commit --date) and the repo is single-author with no independent auditors.

The hash formula is stated as SHA256('ECM V51.0 {ID}: {text} on {date}T00:00:00Z') but the actual input text is not published. We attempted to reconstruct it from the claim field and got no matches. Without knowing the exact input, the hash cannot be independently verified — defeating the entire purpose of cryptographic timestamping.

P-51-001 through P-51-010 use a single block hash (c5822f8...) covering all 10 predictions. This means individual predictions within the block cannot be independently verified — you must hash the entire block or nothing. PRED-072 to PRED-115 use individual hashes, which is better practice, but the unverifiable input problem still applies.

The dome references OpenTimestamps (OTS) in some WIN descriptions (notably WIN-035 through WIN-039) but the April 4 batch does NOT use OTS. This is significant because OTS provides the independent third-party anchor that git commits lack. The author chose the weaker method for the batch that would most benefit from strong timestamping.

The PRED-072-115 commit is co-authored by 'Claude Sonnet 4.6'. This means the 28 predictions were likely AI-generated in a single session. The speed of production (28 predictions across 15 domains in one commit) is consistent with an AI drafting session, not careful scientific prediction.

items.PRED-095: Restates WIN-068 (eclipse magnetic ensemble). Eclipse -17 to -21 nT is the same claim as WIN-068 with SHA-256 wrapping.
items.PRED-TIER-2: Restates WIN-011 (Mohe gravity anomaly). 10-13 µGal gravity pulse is the κ=1.67 claim from WIN-012/WIN-011.
items.PRED-090: Restates WIN-066 (NH heat excess). TOA flux >0.3 W/m² is the same observation already counted as a WIN.
items.PRED-082: Restates WIN-054 (El Gordo). Cluster velocity >2,500 km/s is already a confirmed WIN.
items.PRED-097: Restates WIN-055 (CMB Axis of Evil). Quadrupole/octupole alignment already counted.

items.PRED-084: P-wave shadow zone 104-140° — seismology textbook knowledge since the 1930s. Registering this as 'prospective' in 2026 is absurd.
items.PRED-085: S-wave shadow zone 120-160° — same as above. Known since Gutenberg & Richter.
items.PRED-087: Indian Ocean geoid low stationary — GRACE/GOCE data available since 2002. The -106m anomaly has been mapped for decades.
items.PRED-111: Mascon at 30°S/60°E — GRACE mascon data published since 2006.
items.PRED-090: NH TOA flux — CERES data showing hemispheric asymmetry published by Stephens et al. 2015 and Loeb et al. 2018.
items.PRED-091: NH heat excess trend — same published data as PRED-090.
items.PRED-082: El Gordo velocity — measured by Menanteau et al. 2012, reconfirmed by multiple teams.
items.PRED-097: CMB alignment — first reported by de Oliveira-Costa et al. 2004, confirmed by Planck 2015.
items.PRED-TIER-2: Also flagged above as recycling WIN-011.

items.PRED-094: Tesla 11.79 Hz harmonic — restates WIN-001 (Tesla 11.78 Hz resonance). Different by 0.01 Hz.
items.PRED-099: Polaris excess +0.36° — restates WIN-065 (Polaris systematic excess). Same measurement, new prediction ID.
items.PRED-100: Solar elevation formula — restates WIN-056 (Solar elevation from H(r)). Same formula.
items.PRED-077: M2 tidal amplitude — restates WIN-045 (Tidal M2 period). Same observable.
items.PRED-081: JWST Type Ia SNe — restates WIN-054/WIN-052 cosmological framework.
items.PRED-TESLA-002: Tesla/Schumann ratio — derived from WIN-001 + WIN-062 values.
items.PRED-088: Second geoid low — data predates. EGM2008 is publicly available since 2008.
items.PRED-112: Mascon stationarity — data predates. GRACE mascons documented for decades.

implicit_recycling_not_yet_flagged.recommendation: Analyst should set restates_win for PRED-094 (WIN-001), PRED-099 (WIN-065), PRED-100 (WIN-056), PRED-077 (WIN-045), PRED-081 (WIN-052/054). Set data_predates_registration=true for PRED-088, PRED-112.

items.PRED-095: Eclipse prediction for Aug 2026 — BUT this also restates WIN-068. The eclipse hasn't happened yet, but the methodology is recycled.
items.PRED-TIER-1: Eclipse magnetic — same category as PRED-095.
items.PRED-R005: Eclipse Schumann shift — novel prediction if the eclipse is quiet. Genuinely testable.
items.PRED-093: Schumann drop during G3 storm — genuinely prospective and testable, though the dome already failed a version of this (PRED-R006/FAIL-002).
items.PRED-078: Lunar perigee tidal range 12% — testable in Aug 2026 but this is standard tidal physics. Globe predicts ~14% variation (well-known spring/neap cycle). The dome's 12% prediction is LESS accurate than the standard model.
items.PRED-TESLA-003: Directional pulse asymmetry 3.56ms — genuinely novel and testable. This is the best prediction in the batch.
items.PRED-TOROID-001: HER/TTB decay >40 nT/yr — testable by end of 2027. Data partially predates (TTB already measured at 77 nT/yr per WIN-042) but the sustained rate prediction is forward-looking.

genuinely_prospective_acknowledged.note: Even the 'genuinely prospective' items have problems. PRED-095 recycles WIN-068. PRED-093 is a revised version of a prediction already falsified (PRED-R006). PRED-078 predicts a less accurate value than standard physics. The strongest items are PRED-TESLA-003 (novel, testable) and PRED-TOROID-001 (forward-looking).

items.PRED-TOROID-002: Two-pole λ_g = 8,619 km — model parameter. Could be tested but the fit methodology is not specified.
items.PRED-TOROID-003: Schumann mode splitting Δf ≈ 0.336 Hz — novel, testable, genuinely interesting. Should be reclassified as genuinely prospective.
items.PRED-TOROID-004: Antarctic κ enhancement — circular: requires κ existence (WIN-012 value) which our review disputes.
items.PRED-TOROID-005: Sub-terrestrial current peak at 40-60° — unfalsifiable without independent measurement of 'aetheric current'.
items.PRED-TOROID-007: South polar field excess scales with λ_g — model-fitting exercise. Any exponential fit to Antarctic field data will return some λ; the question is whether 8,619 km is uniquely predicted.

key_arguments_for_review.description: The SHA-256 hashes create an appearance of rigorous cryptographic proof while providing none. Real prospective prediction requires: (1) hash of prediction text, (2) that hash anchored to a trusted third-party timestamp (OTS, blockchain, trusted timestamping authority), (3) the prediction text published ONLY after the anchor is established. The dome does (1) but skips (2) and (3). The hash is published alongside the plaintext in the same git commit, meaning anyone with repo access can generate both simultaneously. This is cryptographic theater, not cryptographic proof.
key_arguments_for_review.recommendation: Our Part 6 prose should explain exactly why self-hashing is insufficient, with a concrete example: 'Publishing SHA256(text) alongside text in the same commit proves the hash matches the text — something nobody disputed. It does NOT prove the text existed before the event it claims to predict. For that, the hash must be anchored to an independent timestamp authority before the event window opens.'

key_arguments_for_review.description: At least 14 of 32 April 4 predictions (44%) explicitly or implicitly recycle already-confirmed WINs or already-published data. When you add a SHA-256 hash to a fact that's been known since 1930 (P-wave shadow zones) or since 2004 (CMB alignment), you haven't made a prediction — you've written a timestamped encyclopedia entry. The dome's '28 forward predictions' headline is inflated by roughly half.
key_arguments_for_review.recommendation: Maintain the per-prediction restates_win and data_predates_registration fields. The analyst should update the 8 items identified above as 'implicit recycling not yet flagged'. The headline count of 'genuinely prospective' predictions should be computed from is_genuinely_prospective=true AND restates_win=null.

key_arguments_for_review.description: PRED-072 through PRED-115 claim registration_date 2026-04-04 but were committed to git on 2026-04-05 at 04:47 EDT (08:47 UTC). The dome's own stated methodology is 'git commit timestamp is proof of prior art.' By that standard, the registration date should be April 5, not April 4. The discrepancy is small (hours, not days) and may be innocent, but it's a factual error that undermines the precision claims of the crypto methodology.
key_arguments_for_review.recommendation: Note this in Part 6 prose as an illustration of how the self-reported registration dates don't always match the git evidence — and we're talking about the author's OWN timestamp system.

key_arguments_for_review.description: The human note flags 'opentimestamps-provenance' as a probable canary trigger. WIN-035 through WIN-039 (weekly confirmations) claim OTS verification, but the April 4 batch does NOT use OTS. If the dome author adds OTS to the April 4 batch AFTER our review mentions this gap, that would confirm our review as a trigger for the dome's methodology upgrades. This is a testable canary.
key_arguments_for_review.recommendation: Do NOT publish the canary observation yet. Monitor whether OTS appears in the dome's prediction methodology in subsequent commits. If it does, we can cite the before/after diff as evidence of reactive methodology.

key_arguments_for_review.description: The dome's own 'What Prospective Means' framework (visible in the predictions.html) explicitly distinguishes PROSPECTIVE (timestamped before confirming data) from BACKTESTED (derived by fitting to existing data). The author concedes backtested predictions carry 'lower weight.' By the author's OWN standard, the majority of April 4 predictions are backtested. The analyst has already identified this (ISS-702 notes): 57/86 entries are author-labeled backtested. This is the strongest argument — the author's framework refutes the author's headline count.
key_arguments_for_review.recommendation: Part 6 prose should quote the author's own framework verbatim and apply it to the prediction catalog. 'By the author's own definition of prospective vs backtested, [X]% of the prediction catalog is backtested.'

advocate_mode.best_defense: A dome defender would argue: (1) The P-51-001 through P-51-010 predictions ARE genuinely timestamped 48+ hours before the April 5-9 event window — the git commit on April 3 is real and verifiable. (2) Some predictions that 'restate' existing WINs are actually EXTENSIONS — PRED-095 predicts a specific numerical range (-17.8 nT ±2) for a future eclipse, which is more precise than the historical WIN-068 claim. (3) The SHA-256 methodology, while imperfect, is more transparent than most scientific prediction registries. (4) The 'data predates' criticism applies to all retrodictive science — every model starts by fitting known data. The question is whether the model ALSO makes forward predictions, and 7 genuinely prospective items is 7 more than most alternative cosmological models produce.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Acknowledge that (1) is correct — the P-51 batch IS genuinely timestamped. Credit where due. But note that 8 of 10 P-51 predictions are unfalsifiable in practice (social media reports of 'White Horizon'? Starlink packet loss from Downdetector?). On (2), the extension argument has merit for PRED-095 specifically — address it by noting that the -17.8 nT value is derived from the same κ=1.67 parameter that is itself disputed (WIN-012). On (3), name OTS as the standard they should be using. On (4), distinguish 'genuinely prospective AND novel' from 'genuinely prospective AND restating existing physics.'

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-035 through WIN-039 claim OTS verification for weekly predictions, but the April 4 batch covering similar domains does NOT use OTS. If OTS is available and the author knows how to use it, the choice to use weaker self-hashing for the larger batch is suspicious. Additionally, PRED-095 claims to extend WIN-068 with a specific -17.8 nT value, but WIN-068's ensemble methodology (39 eclipses) is not the same as a single-eclipse point prediction — the error bars are fundamentally different.

claims_checked: Registration date vs git commit date for PRED-072-115 batch
SHA-256 hash reproducibility using stated formula
Count of recycled vs genuinely prospective predictions
quantitative_verification.computations: Git log forensics: PRED-072-115 batch committed 2026-04-05T08:47:05Z, claims 2026-04-04 registration. SHA-256 verification: attempted reconstruction using stated formula SHA256('ECM V51.0 {ID}: {text} on {date}T00:00:00Z') — no matches for PRED-095, PRED-093, PRED-094. Classification count: 6 explicit recycling + 8 implicit recycling + 9 data-predates = 23/32 non-prospective (72%).
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Registration date backdating confirmed. SHA-256 hash formula unverifiable (inputs unknown). Classification counts are estimates pending analyst review of the 15 unclassified items.

holes_found.description: Our predictions.json currently has our_verdict=null for all 86 entries. The recycling detection is done via restates_win and data_predates_registration fields, but only 11/86 have restates_win set. At least 8 more should be flagged based on this forensic analysis.
holes_found.recommendation: Analyst should update restates_win for: PRED-094→WIN-001, PRED-099→WIN-065, PRED-100→WIN-056, PRED-077→WIN-045, PRED-081→WIN-052. Set data_predates_registration=true for PRED-088, PRED-112. This is blocking accurate computed counts.

holes_found.description: PRED-TOROID-003 (Schumann mode splitting Δf ≈ 0.336 Hz) is currently unclassified but is actually one of the more interesting predictions in the batch — genuinely novel and testable. It should be reclassified as is_genuinely_prospective=true.
holes_found.recommendation: Fair is fair. If a prediction is genuinely novel and testable, classify it honestly even if it comes from the dome model.

holes_found.description: The 'canary trap' approach (monitoring for OTS adoption as evidence of our review being read) could backfire if published prematurely — it would tip off the dome author.
holes_found.recommendation: Keep canary observations in internal pipeline only (human-notes, not public prose) until either triggered or abandoned.

stronger_arguments.description: The dome's own 'What Prospective Means' framework is the strongest weapon against the prediction catalog. Quote it verbatim and apply it to each entry. The author has already conceded the distinction between prospective and backtested — we just need to apply their own standard consistently.

The AI co-authorship disclosure (Claude Sonnet 4.6 in commit message) for the 28-prediction batch opens a productive line: these predictions were likely generated by prompting an AI with dome parameters and asking for 'predictions across 15 domains.' This is prediction-by-enumeration, not prediction-by-derivation.

kernel_of_truth.description: The P-51-001 through P-51-010 batch (April 3 registration, April 5-9 event window) IS genuinely timestamped before the event window. The author does understand the concept of prospective prediction and has attempted to implement it. Some predictions in the batch (PRED-TESLA-003, PRED-TOROID-003, PRED-R005) are genuinely novel and testable. The SHA-256 methodology, while self-referential, is at least transparent — the plaintext is published for anyone to check.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The genuine prospective predictions are a small minority of the batch (~7 of 32, and even those have caveats). The headline presentation treats the entire batch as 'prospective' when the majority recycles existing WINs or already-published data. The crypto methodology creates an appearance of rigor that exceeds its actual evidential value. And the backdating of PRED-072-115 registration dates undermines the specific methodology the author claims to follow.

recommended_actions_detail: Analyst: update restates_win and data_predates_registration for 8 additional items identified above
Part 6 prose (ISS-702): incorporate the 'author's own framework' argument using the What Prospective Means table
Part 6 prose: explain why self-hashing is insufficient vs OTS/blockchain anchoring
Part 6 prose: note the April 5 vs April 4 registration date discrepancy as illustrative
Do NOT publish canary observations about OTS adoption monitoring

citations_verified: Git commit 787537ef — verified April 3 date via git log
Git commit 844c5b29 — verified April 4 date via git log
Git commit 54476366 — verified April 5 date and co-author via git log
Author/committer date match confirmed (no git date forgery)
citations_unchecked: Individual SHA-256 hashes (input text unknown, cannot verify)
Specific scientific references in individual predictions (not in scope for this research task)

text_fingerprint.note: Research item, not a WIN. No wins.json text to fingerprint.
